sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citation occurrences resolve to the named pages or PDF, including the GovDash page after following its www-hosted result.

figures_match_sources.note: All seven citations have material scope or figure gaps: the sources do not establish the record's 541715-specific consolidator count, 6.5x/12.0x R&D-services multiples, 22% US defense-R&D adoption, 12-13-year CAD diffusion, 40% age-60+ ownership and succession shortage, or the asserted 60-65% CPFF billing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Individual within-role fractions are bounded and directionally plausible, but inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role sums to only 0.90 of labor while its own reviewer_note admits 10% is unaccounted; the omitted labor is score-affecting. pi_dist=0.30 and t50_years=8.0 also depend on unsupported billing-mix and adoption/analog anchors.

rubric_consistent.note: The BORDERLINE flag is unresolved because S=2.87779338 is only 0.1222 from the 3.00 pass cut and central estimates are not source-supported. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is unresolved: active_consolidators, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult and owners_60plus_pct are all estimates, and their cited pages do not validate the industry-specific values. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag does not itself invalidate the record, but tolerance-level deltas on B, M, S and V cannot cure the provenance and decomposition defects.

cross_checks_honest.note: The four mandatory cross-checks are present and no-proxy is stated, but the adoption and CAD evidence used for A does not support the stated US-defense-R&D figures, while pricing_model_deflation repeats an unsupported 60-65% CPFF / 15-20% SBIR / 15-20% commercial mix; the cross-checks therefore overstate what the evidence establishes.

source_audits.note: The January 2025 Janes article reports five broad aerospace-and-defense PE buyers and their acquisitions through 2024-06-30 (including 17, 10, 13, 12 and 12 add-ons). It does not identify eight platforms active specifically in 541715 engineering/applied-R&D services, and its measurement window does not establish activity in the 24 months ending 2026-07-20.

The marketing firm's Q1 2025 table reports 7.7x for broad Military & Defense companies at $1-3M EBITDA and 10.6x at $3-5M. It does not report a 6.5x median for 541715 SBIR/cost-plus R&D shops across $1-10M EBITDA or document the claimed post-owner-comp normalization and affiliation-risk adjustment.

The OECD report supports more than 25% AI use in the broad Professional, scientific and technical activities sector and a 13-point 2021-2024 increase, using cross-country/Eurostat sector evidence. It does not establish 22% material adoption for US 541715 defense R&D or the quoted BCG two-thirds proof-of-concept assertion.

The page reports 14.7x for broad Military & Defense companies at $5-15M EBITDA and says defense contracting averaged about 12x overall. It does not establish a 12.0x transaction/comps multiple for $10M+ EBITDA government R&D-services platforms or the claimed services-versus-product discount.

The secondary CAD history supports Autodesk's 1982 founding and says the 1980s brought large-scale CAD adoption, but it provides no >50% penetration figure for the mid-1990s and no evidence for a 12-13-year time-to-50%; it also does not support the separate ERP 10-12-year analog.

The April 2026 McKinsey chart says over half of all small-business owners are over 55 and roughly six million SMB transitions are expected by 2035. It does not report 40% of 541715 owners age 60+, one in four owners age 65+, 62% accelerated retirements, fewer than one-third having succession plans, or a defense-R&D-specific succession shortage.

The April 2026 guide supports CPFF reimbursement of allowable costs, a fixed fee, DCAA/FAR Part 31 tracking and the 15% R&D fee cap. It does not establish that 60-65% of 541715 revenue is CPFF, the 15-20% SBIR and commercial/FFP shares, SBIR award caps, or the quoted $599.8B DCAA examination figure.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators (https://blog.janescapital.com/key-insights/top-5-most-active-aerospace-defense-private-equity-buyers-2022-2024), the cited broad A&D top-five buyer list through 2024-06-30 does not support value=8 active 541715 add-on platforms in the last 24 months. Re-run with dated platform/add-on evidence specific to engineering/applied-R&D services and enumerate the counted platforms.

At inputs.buy_mult (https://firstpagesage.com/business/aerospace-ebitda-valuation-multiples/), the source reports broad Military & Defense multiples of 7.7x at $1-3M and 10.6x at $3-5M EBITDA, not the score-affecting 6.5x median for $1-10M 541715 firms. Re-run with actual small-firm transaction or credible comp evidence on post-market-owner-comp EBITDA and a documented conversion/adjustment chain.

At inputs.exit_mult (https://firstpagesage.com/business/aerospace-ebitda-valuation-multiples/), the source does not establish 12.0x for $10M+ government R&D-services platforms. Re-run with recent services-platform transactions or directly comparable public/private comps and document scope, date and EBITDA normalization.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct (https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2025/12/ai-adoption-by-small-and-medium-sized-enterprises_9c48eae6/426399c1-en.pdf), broad cross-country professional-services evidence does not establish 22% material adoption in US 541715, and the same URL does not support the quoted BCG assertion. Re-run with accessible US/industry evidence for each anchor and a transparent adjustment to the industry value.

At inputs.historical_analogs (https://www.scan2cad.com/blog/cad/cad-evolved-since-1982/), the source does not report >50% CAD penetration by the mid-1990s or the asserted 12-13-year diffusion period, and no auditable ERP source is supplied. Re-run with measured time-to-50% evidence for each analog before deriving inputs.t50_years.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct and inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented (https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/week-in-charts/boomers-and-the-business-baton), the source supports only broad over-55 ownership and expected transition volume, not value=0.40 at age 60+ or a documented 541715 succession shortage. Re-run with age-threshold-compatible owner data and explicit evidence of a shortage before applying the OW succession bonus.

At inputs.pricing_structure and inputs.pi_dist (https://govdash.com/blog/cpff-contracts-guide), the source explains CPFF mechanics but not the score-affecting 541715 billing shares. Re-run with contract-award or industry revenue evidence for the CPFF/fixed-price/commercial mix, then derive pi_dist and the pricing cross-check from that supported mix.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, labor_share_of_total sums to 0.90 while reviewer_note admits 10% is unaccounted, so the role-weighted V input is incomplete. Re-run with a mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive labor allocation summing to 1.0 and concrete rationales for the added residual roles.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 5 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V
